what_it_does: CSS token sheet that defines the Fresh Paper design language for the surface app: a comprehensive set of :root custom properties for color, typography, spacing, elevation, radii, and safe areas, plus global reset/base rules and reusable .ui-indicated / .ui-spotlight accent classes.
observations: Defines semantic CSS custom properties under :root using names like --ground-*, --ink-*, --pig-*, --elev-*, --t-*, --s-*, --r-*.
Includes taste toggles via :root[data-type] and :root[data-pigment] that swap typeface pairings and pigment saturation.
Sets html/body/#root to full height/width, warm paper background, overflow hidden, and antialiased text.
Provides .ui-indicated and .ui-spotlight classes with a single-shot ping animation using cubic-bezier ease.
Uses env(safe-area-inset-*) for iOS PWA safe areas.
Contains a heavy-handed prefers-reduced-motion media query that zeroes all animation/transition durations globally.
Comments reference external concepts not present in the file: rhm.css .vhandle-scrim, DNA's steer, mandate L7, S4, etc.
standouts: The single-shot spotlight ping animation is explicitly designed not to loop, settling to a steady ring to avoid an alarm feel.
The file declares a deliberate anti-neon-on-black sci-fi aesthetic in favor of warm paper and diffuse elevation.
z-index 45 is assigned to .ui-spotlight so it sits above a referenced voice-conversation scrim but below a V panel.
conventions: CSS custom properties as the single source of truth for component styling.
Semantic token naming grouped by function: ground, ink, pigment, elevation, type, spacing, radii.
Modular type scale based on 1.25 Major Third and dyadic spacing increments.
Global box-sizing reset and base typography applied to html, body, and #root.
Reduced-motion guard at the platform level via media query.
concerns: Comments rely on external files and project-specific terms (rhm.css, DNA's steer, V panel z 46) that can drift without this file knowing.
z-index 45 in a utility class may create unexpected stacking interactions if other layers change.
The reduced-motion rule applies * { animation-duration/transition-duration: 0.001ms !important }, which is global and could suppress intended transitions elsewhere.
html/body/#root overflow: hidden assumes the app owns all scrolling and may conflict with standard page behavior.
The spotlight keyframes animate box-shadow only; while acceptable, it is a paint-heavy property to animate.
notes: This is the central Fresh Paper token sheet and base reset for the surface UI, defining the visual language variables and a calm, single-shot spotlight accent.
questions: Which components consume these tokens, and how are the taste toggles triggered at runtime?
What are the exact z-index layer assignments for the referenced V panel and rhm.css scrim?
How are the pigment names (born, pile, strain, leanA, leanB) mapped to specific UI states or statuses?
Is the 0.001ms reduced-motion override scoped globally by design, or should it be limited to this file's animations?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to be a wholesale design-language token layer that components read without hardcoded values; the file fulfills that for token definitions and also adds base reset rules and UI accent classes, so it does slightly more than pure tokens.
apparent_themes: Restrained color used as information scent
Accessibility and high contrast (WCAG AAA claim)
Motion that resolves to a stable end frame
intention_signals: It appears to be trying to establish a cohesive, themeable, non-jarring visual system for an operator or voice-assisted surface, with a clear but calm attention marker for guided interactions.
novelty: The explicit anti-sci-fi paper mandate, semantic pigment names tied to lifecycle or state concepts (born/pile/strain/lean), and a single-shot ping spotlight orchestrated around a voice-conversation scrim.
contribution: A centralized set of CSS custom-property tokens and base rules that a fused system could keep as a shared theme layer, plus a reusable spotlight accent pattern and a reduced-motion guard.
summary_for_embedding: CSS design-token file defining the Fresh Paper visual language for a surface app, including color, typography, spacing, elevation, radii, safe-area variables, taste toggles, base reset, and a single-shot spotlight indicator.
intention_for_embedding: To provide a single, themeable source of truth for warm-paper UI styling and a calm, accessible attention marker.
